evidence.OWNER_GATE: Any change to frozen contracts/seeds/KPI after baseline; any real shipping or payment integration
evidence.ENGINEERING_REQUIRED: If variant-cart schema cannot be represented in commerce-core without schema changes — stop and surface
nonclaims: does not prove real printing or shipping
does not prove real artist payment
synthetic cohort only — not real sticker buyer behavior
physical fulfillment is simulated — no real logistics

required_inputs.goal_statement: Build Marginalia — a local synthetic sticker storefront running the full WEAVE lifecycle (intent→analysis→seal). The goal is to produce reproducible lifecycle evidence for a distinct physical-goods domain (curated sticker drops with variants, cart, fulfillment tracking, and cancellation), proving WEAVE operates a product lifecycle end-to-end for a second app type distinct from OneReel.
required_inputs.target_user: Planner/journal hobbyist — treats their notebook as a daily ritual; wants a few beautiful sticker packs from real artists, not ten thousand near-identical marketplace results. Frustrated by: marketplace flood (confusing searches), mystery quality, misleading mockups, unparseable variant menus (size/finish/sheet-count). Persona source: atm-418-brand.md + contracts/specs/sticker-storefront.spec.json.
success_criteria: npm run build exits 0, zero console errors
impeccable critique ≥ 7/10, zero P0 findings on buyer-facing surface
deterministic baseline cohort (seed=42, 200 users) produces consistent kpi.json across ≥2 runs
one bounded adaptation applied and retested with identical seed — KPI delta recorded
seal manifest produced with file checksums
conversion_rate and aov measurable; refund_rate guardrail < 0.15 held
non_goals: No real printing, shipping, or artist contracts
No real payment processing or provider integration
No public deployment, marketing, or user contact
No copyrighted artwork (all sticker art is synthetic CSS shapes)
deployment and marketing lifecycle stages are owner-gated-skipped per ENVELOPE.md

OWNER_GATE: Any change to frozen contracts/schemas/seeds/KPI/state-transitions after baseline freeze
Any real shipping, payment, artist-contract, or deployment action
Any change to the negative contract or public-safe requirements
ENGINEERING_REQUIRED: If commerce-core cannot represent variant-cart schema (size × finish × sheet-count) without schema changes

goal_clarity.evidence: Goal is specific (local synthetic sticker storefront, distinct from OneReel) and testable (build gate, validate-contracts gate, QA gate, deterministic cohort). Outcome is 'sealed Marginalia app with lifecycle proof artifacts'.

target_user.evidence: Planner/journal hobbyist persona is named, distinct from OneReel's filmmaker-patron, with a specific frustration (marketplace flood, variant opacity) and a clear use case (buy a few curated physical packs at true size before paying).

success_measures.evidence: Primary KPI conversion_rate and secondary aov are numeric and measurable via cohort runner. Guardrail refund_rate < 0.15 is a hard threshold. Cohort is frozen (seed=42, 200 users). Build and QA gates are binary pass/fail.

scope_boundaries.evidence: Non-goals are explicit (no real shipping/payment/deployment). Approval boundaries named: OWNER_GATE for frozen-contract changes and any real external action; ENGINEERING_REQUIRED for schema gaps. Nonclaims committed in spec.
